an1.306:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise, or by which arisen unwholesome *dhammas* grow and expand, as much as wrong view.
an1.306:1.2: In one with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* grow and expand."
an1.307:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise, or by which arisen wholesome *dhammas* grow and expand, as much as right view.
an1.307:1.2: In one with right view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* grow and expand."
an1.308:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wholesome *dhammas* do not arise, or by which arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline, as much as wrong view.
an1.308:1.2: In one with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.309:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* do not arise, or by which arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline, as much as right view.
an1.309:1.2: In one with right view, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* do not arise, and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.310:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen wrong view arises, or by which arisen wrong view grows, as much as inappropriate attention [*ayoniso manasikāra*].
an1.310:1.2: In one who attends inappropriately [*ayoniso manasikāra*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen wrong view arises, and arisen wrong view grows."
an1.311:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which unarisen right view arises, or by which arisen right view grows, as much as appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*].
an1.311:1.2: In one who attends appropriately [*yoniso manasikāra*], *bhikkhus*, unarisen right view arises, and arisen right view grows."
an1.312:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which beings, at the breaking up of the body, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad destination, a state of ruin, in hell, as much as wrong view.
an1.312:1.2: Beings endowed with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, at the breaking up of the body, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad destination, a state of ruin, in hell."
an1.313:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, by which beings, at the breaking up of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world, as much as right view.
an1.313:1.2: Beings endowed with right view, *bhikkhus*, at the breaking up of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world."
an1.314:1.1: "For a person with wrong view, *bhikkhus*, whatever bodily *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever intentions, whatever wishes, whatever resolves, whatever *saṅkhāras* — all these *dhammas* lead to what is unwanted, unloved, disagreeable, to harm and *dukkha*.
an1.314:1.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a seed of neem, or of bitter gourd, or of bitter bottle-gourd planted in moist soil — whatever essence of earth it takes up and whatever essence of water it takes up, all of it tends toward bitterness, pungency, and unpleasantness.
an1.314:1.7: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, for a person with wrong view, whatever bodily *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever intentions, whatever wishes, whatever resolves, whatever *saṅkhāras* — all these *dhammas* lead to what is unwanted, unloved, disagreeable, to harm and *dukkha*.
an1.315:1.1: "For a person with right view, *bhikkhus*, whatever bodily *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever intentions, whatever wishes, whatever resolves, whatever *saṅkhāras* — all these *dhammas* lead to what is wanted, loved, agreeable, to benefit and *sukha*.
an1.315:1.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a seed of sugarcane, or of rice, or of grape planted in moist soil — whatever essence of earth it takes up and whatever essence of water it takes up, all of it tends toward sweetness, pleasantness, and delightful flavour.
an1.315:1.7: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, for a person with right view, whatever bodily *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever verbal *kamma* … whatever mental *kamma* is undertaken and carried out in accordance with that view, whatever intentions, whatever wishes, whatever resolves, whatever *saṅkhāras* — all these *dhammas* lead to what is wanted, loved, agreeable, to benefit and *sukha*.
